mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, which drives corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or forward returns are negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and CPIAUCSL growth stays below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved corporate output and margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 70% of recovery-phase observations, or the signal IC turns negative during supply-chain normalization.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on inflation surprises above 0.4% MoM, or the negative return pattern disappears when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns when payrolls rise but unemployment also rises, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, which precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter as financial and consumer sectors face headwinds.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline coincides with positive 60-day equity returns in greater than 60% of late-cycle observations, or the predictive relationship vanishes when mortgage rates are below 5%.
